sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal. Arab. and Eth. to twist, to twine, to spin. See פָּתִיל .

Niphal-

(1) to be twisted; metaph. to be crafty, deceitful, Pro 8:8 Job 5:13.

(2) to wrestle (which is done by twisting the limbs together), Gen 30:8. See נַפְתּוּלִים .

Hithpael, to act perversely or deceitfully, Psa 18:27. As to the form תִּתַּפַּל 2Sa 22:27, see Analyt. Ind.

Derivatives, פָּתִיל, נַפְתּוּלִים pr.n. נַפְתָּלְי, and-
